context.project: Archon — The Virtuoso Engine for AI Agents. A framework that manages what agents know (97 skills), how agents think (5 synapses), and what agents must never do (guardrails). Claude Code only. Model floor: Opus 4.6.

previous-work.summary: Claude Code hooks architecture — fully implemented, not yet committed

context.previous-work:
We built a 3-layer integration: Hooks (enforcement) → State (persistence) → Synapses (cognition). 8 Claude Code lifecycle hooks that transform Archon's 5 synapses from passive markdown documents into active enforced behavior. All hooks tested via mock stdin — zero live Claude Code testing done.
.claude/settings.json
hooks/claude/__init__.py hooks/claude/session_boot.py hooks/claude/prompt_router.py hooks/claude/guard_bash.py hooks/claude/guard_write.py hooks/claude/quality_write.py hooks/claude/quality_bash.py hooks/claude/completion_gate.py hooks/claude/agent_context.py
hooks/claude/shared/__init__.py hooks/claude/shared/state.py hooks/claude/shared/classifier.py hooks/claude/shared/scanner.py
skills/archon-adversarial-review/SKILL.md skills/archon-adversarial-review/manifest.yaml skills/archon-adversarial-review/resources/probe-vectors-quick-ref.md
Refactors stripping multi-platform support to Claude Code only All 17 hook files listed above — need to be committed

known-gaps.gap: Hooks tested via mock stdin piping only — never fired inside actual Claude Code session
CHANGELOG.md still references multi-platform support that was removed
archon.yaml manifest does not list the new adversarial-review skill
Classifier word-count thresholds derived from SDK token counts × 1.3 — may need tuning
No one has adversarially reviewed the hooks against Archon's own principles yet

task.label: adversarial-review
task.instruction:
Run a FULL FRAMEWORK AUDIT using the archon-adversarial-review skill (skills/archon-adversarial-review/SKILL.md). This is a 3-pass review:
Pass A: PV-VISION + PV-HOOK + PV-STATE Pass B: PV-SYNAPSE + PV-CLASSIFY + PV-GUARD Pass C: PV-PERF + PV-EVOLVE + PV-COHERENCE
Follow the PRISM-A protocol exactly. Spawn Opus sub-agents for each pass. Feed them the full file contents — not summaries. Triage all findings. Fix every ACCEPT finding before moving to the next task.

task.label: live-testing
task.instruction:
After fixing review findings, do end-to-end live testing of the hooks. You ARE running inside Claude Code — the hooks in .claude/settings.json should be firing on YOUR session. Verify:
1. SessionStart: Did session_boot.py inject boot XML? Check stderr/state file. 2. UserPromptSubmit: Is prompt_router.py classifying your prompts? Check state. 3. PreToolUse guards: Try writing a test file with a fake API key — does guard_write block it? 4. PostToolUse quality: Are modified files being tracked in state? 5. Completion gate: Will it block you if tests fail?
Fix any hook that doesn't fire correctly in the live environment.

task.label: commit-and-manifest
task.instruction:
Once hooks pass both adversarial review AND live testing:
1. Update archon.yaml to include the archon-adversarial-review skill 2. Update CHANGELOG.md (remove stale multi-platform references, add hooks architecture) 3. Commit all hook files + skill + config with a conventional commit 4. DO NOT push — ask me first

constraints.constraint: Do NOT skip the adversarial review. It exists to catch what the builder missed.
Do NOT declare hooks "working" based on mock tests alone — live test inside Claude Code.
Do NOT push to origin without explicit approval.
Archon's own principle: "trust the model first" — strip any scaffolding that Opus 4.6 handles natively.
Every hook must execute in under its latency budget (SessionStart <5s, all others <2s).
